texts.full_text: range of hands-on exploration of interactive technologies and creative activities, such as contributing to a ‘virtual quilt’ and working with heat sensitive dyes, which were useful for generating ideas for lessons. It also enabled sharing of ideas through group discussion around a range of creative technology issues. Further face-to-face discussion with participants alloWed for the dissemination of ideas, experiences and knowledge. A range of presentations by schools, students and professionals showcased the application of a number of creative technologies, providing the bigger picture that could be used to inspire and inform students about creative applications and possible career paths involving creative technologies. Areas covered included wearable light sensors, computer science and engineering opportunities, app design, the creation of virtual environments, code clubs and social-interactive design projects. Nicolas Gold, Computer Science, University College London As a

texts.full_text: list = web.Lists["Suppliers"]; SPListItem item = list.AddItem(); item[SPBuiltInFieldId.Title] = "Test"; item["Product"] = 1; // Use the ID; get it from the list item.Update(); } In this case, I have a list called Suppliers which has a field called Product that is a lookup to a list called Products. You cAn use the ID of the lookup target in either integer form or in string form ("1"). I've hardcoded it just to provide an example. You'll need to do a separate lookup to the target list to get the value. A: A better way of doing this is the SPFieldlookupValue class. MSD

texts.preview_tail: bal warming if they escape during use or disposal). - Higher COP (Coefficient Of Performance) leading to more energy efficiency due lower running costs & greater lifespan of parts such as compressors,
texts.full_text: magnetic working substance. The magnetic working substance is shaped into minute spheres and is filled in a work chamber. A magnetic filed is alternately impressed to or removed from the magnetic working substance. Heat medium is made to reciprocate in the work chamber, synchronizing with the impression and removal of the magnetic field, so that the magnetic heat pump apparatus achieves heat transport. Specific surface area of the magnetic working substance is increased due to the minute spherical sBased on this information about a "magnetic" or magnetocaloric cooling device: User: It seems you are describing an application for adiabatic demagnetization in coolers and refrigerators, right? Can these tiny beads of magnetic working substance help make portable air conditioning units more energy-efficient by lowering their operating temperatures compared to conventional compressors like the Rankine cycle with HFCs or CFCs as a circulating fluid. System: Yes exactly! This kind of "magnetic cooling" can indeed improve efficiency and is considered an environmentally friendly alternative, known for its potential use in residential air conditioning systems too (not just portable ones). The beads could effectively serve the same purpose that HFCs/CFS have served before by acting as a heat exchange medium. Here're some notable benefits of magnetic cooling: - No CCF gases means less environmental impact since these are potent greenhouse emissions. They can significantly reduce global warming if they escape during use or disposal). - Higher COP (Coefficient Of Performance) leading to more energy efficiency due lower running costs & greater lifespan of parts such as compressors,
